description: Core Linux commands for DevOps workflows

categories.name: File System
categories.description: Navigate, inspect, and manipulate the file system

commands.name: ls
commands.summary: List directory contents

options.description: Long listing format with permissions, size, date
Sort by modification time, newest first
Sort by file size, largest first

examples.description: Long listing with hidden files and readable sizes
Sort by most recently modified

commands.name: find
commands.summary: Search for files and directories by name, type, size, date, or content

options.description: Run a command on each result

examples.description: Find all .log files in /var modified in last 24h
Find files larger than 500MB
Find and delete temp files older than 7 days

commands.name: du
commands.summary: Estimate disk usage of files and directories

options.description: Summarize: only total for each argument
Limit depth of directory tree to N levels

examples.description: Show top-level directory sizes in /var
Total size of current directory

commands.name: df
commands.summary: Report file system disk space usage

options.description: Show inode usage instead of block usage

examples.description: Check disk space usage of all mounted file systems
Check inode usage to find if a file system is full of small files

commands.name: cache
commands.summary: Clear file system caches to free memory or test performance

examples.description: Clear all file system caches (requires root)

commands.name: chmod
commands.summary: Change file mode (permissions)

options.description: rw-r--r-- — owner rw, others read only

examples.description: Make a script executable
Set standard web file permissions recursively

commands.name: chown
commands.summary: Change file owner and group

examples.description: Give app user ownership of a directory

categories.name: Search and Navigation
categories.description: Find content inside files and navigate the system quickly

commands.name: grep
commands.summary: Search text patterns in files or streams

options.description: Invert match — show lines NOT matching
Show N lines before and after match

examples.description: Find ERROR lines in all logs, show 3 lines context
Find a process in ps output
examples.code: ps aux | grep nginx | grep -v grep

examples.description: Search for IP addresses with regex

commands.name: tail
commands.summary: View the end of a file; follow live log output

options.description: Follow: stream new lines as they're written
Like -f but retry if file is rotated/missing

examples.description: Live-stream an application log
Watch last 100 lines of nginx access log
Follow multiple files at once

commands.name: awk
commands.summary: Pattern scanning and processing — extract columns, transform text

options.description: Built-in: number of fields in line

examples.description: Print the 2nd column of a space-delimited file
Print username and shell from /etc/passwd
Sum the values in column 3

commands.name: sed
commands.summary: Stream editor — find and replace text in files or streams

options.description: Substitute all occurrences of old with new

examples.description: Replace all occurrences in a file
Delete lines containing a pattern
Print lines 10 to 20

categories.name: Process Management
categories.description: Monitor, control, and troubleshoot running processes

commands.name: ps
commands.summary: Report a snapshot of current processes

options.description: All users, full details, BSD syntax (most common)

examples.description: Find a specific process
Show top memory consumers
Show process tree

commands.name: kill / killall
commands.summary: Send a signal to terminate or control processes

options.description: SIGKILL — force kill immediately, no cleanup

examples.description: Gracefully stop a process by PID
Force-kill a hung process
Kill all processes named nginx

commands.note: Prefer SIGTERM (-15) over SIGKILL (-9) to allow graceful shutdown.

commands.name: top / htop
commands.summary: Interactive real-time process monitor

options.description: Show only processes for a specific user
Set refresh interval to N seconds
Update N times then exit (for scripting)

examples.description: Monitor a specific process by PID
One-shot CPU/mem snapshot (non-interactive)

commands.name: lsof
commands.summary: List open files — identify which process has a file, port, or socket open

options.description: Show processes listening on a port
Show files opened by a PID
Show files opened by a user
Show files opened by processes matching name

examples.description: Who is using port 8080?
What files does a process have open?
Find deleted files still held open (disk leak)

categories.name: Networking
categories.description: Diagnose connectivity, inspect traffic, and manage network interfaces

commands.name: curl
commands.summary: Transfer data from/to a server using various protocols

options.description: Write output to FILE instead of stdout
Specify HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)

examples.description: Check HTTP status code of a URL
POST JSON to an API endpoint
Download a file and follow redirects
Measure response time breakdown

commands.name: ss / netstat
commands.summary: Display socket and network connection statistics

examples.description: Show all listening TCP ports with process names
Show established connections
Count connections per remote IP
examples.code: ss -tn | awk '{print $5}' | cut -d: -f1 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

commands.name: tcpdump
commands.summary: Capture and analyze network packets on an interface

options.description: Capture on specific interface (e.g. eth0, any)
Write raw packets to file (for Wireshark)
Capture only N packets then exit

examples.description: Capture HTTP traffic on port 80
Capture and save to file for analysis
Capture traffic between two hosts

commands.note: Requires root or CAP_NET_ADMIN. Use -c to limit packet count in production.

categories.name: System Diagnostics
categories.description: Investigate system health, resource usage, and kernel events

commands.name: journalctl
commands.summary: Query and display systemd journal logs

options.description: Follow: stream new log entries live
Show entries since time (e.g. '1 hour ago')
Filter by priority (err, warn, info, debug)

examples.description: Follow nginx logs live
Show errors from the last hour
Show logs from last boot for a service

commands.name: dmesg
commands.summary: Read kernel ring buffer messages — hardware, driver, and boot events

examples.description: Show recent kernel errors with timestamps
Monitor kernel messages in real time
Check for OOM (Out of Memory) kills

commands.name: vmstat
commands.summary: Report virtual memory, CPU, I/O statistics in real time

examples.description: Report every 2 seconds, 10 times
Check memory summary

commands.name: strace
commands.summary: Trace system calls made by a process — essential for deep debugging

options.description: Attach to a running process by PID
Filter to specific syscall types (e.g. file, network)
Count calls and show summary statistics

examples.description: Trace file system calls of a running app
Run a command and trace all syscalls
Get syscall count summary

commands.note: strace adds overhead. Avoid on high-traffic production processes.
